name: Sigyn
description: Sigyn, in Norse mythology, is a figure primarily known for her unwavering loyalty and devotion to her husband, Loki, even in the face of his transgressions against the gods. Her name, often interpreted as "victorious friend" or "friend of victory," ironically contrasts with the suffering she endures due to Loki's actions. While her origins and parentage remain largely obscure, Sigyn's significance lies primarily in her role during Loki's punishment. Following Loki's involvement in Baldr's death, the gods imprisoned him in a cave, binding him with the entrails of his son Narfi. Skadi, seeking vengeance, placed a venomous serpent above Loki, its venom dripping onto him. Sigyn, demonstrating unparalleled fidelity, sits beside him, tirelessly catching the venom in a bowl. When the bowl fills, she must empty it, causing the venom to drip onto Loki, resulting in agonizing pain that causes him to writhe and convulse, creating earthquakes. Sigyn's profound commitment to mitigating Loki's suffering highlights a complex theme of love, duty, and endurance within Norse cosmology. She is a powerful example of devotion in the face of unbearable circumstances.
subtitle: The Loyal Wife

keyMyths.title: Loki's Imprisonment and Sigyn's Devotion
keyMyths.description: Following Loki's role in the death of Baldr, the gods punish him severely. He is bound in a cave with the entrails of his son, and a venomous serpent is placed above him. Sigyn remains by his side, catching the venom in a bowl to alleviate his suffering. This act of unwavering devotion defines her character and highlights the depth of her commitment to Loki, even in the face of his crimes against the gods. Her actions serve as a testament to enduring love.
keyMyths.source: The Prose Edda, Gylfaginning and Skáldskaparmál

keyMyths.title: The Cause of Earthquakes
keyMyths.description: The myth surrounding Loki's imprisonment also provides an etiological explanation for earthquakes. When Sigyn must empty the bowl filled with venom, the poison drips onto Loki, causing him immense pain. His writhing and convulsions are said to be the source of earthquakes in the mortal realm, connecting the divine punishment to natural phenomena and illustrating the interconnectedness of the cosmos within Norse belief.

keyMyths.title: Sigyn's Silent Suffering
keyMyths.description: While the myth focuses on Loki's pain and punishment, Sigyn's own suffering is often overlooked. She is condemned to an eternity of witnessing and attempting to alleviate Loki's torment. Her silence and unwavering dedication implicitly highlight the personal cost of loyalty and the potential for quiet suffering to accompany acts of devotion. This aspect of her character adds a layer of complexity to her portrayal.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, providing the most detailed account of Sigyn's role in Loki's punishment.

sources.author: Hilda Ellis Davidson
sources.work: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe

sources.author: John Lindow
